canonical_definition: The phenomenon of producing different, often incorrect, answers across multiple attempts at solving the same problem under identical conditions.
papers: ECODecoding: Entropy-Based Control for Controllability and Fluency in Controllable Dialogue Generation
SCRIBE: Structured Chain Reasoning for Interactive Behaviour Explanations using Tool Calling

definitions.text: The phenomenon of producing different, often incorrect, answers across multiple attempts at solving the same problem under identical conditions.
definitions.paper: ECODecoding: Entropy-Based Control for Controllability and Fluency in Controllable Dialogue Generation

definitions.text: Producing logically inconsistent outputs (e.g., both 'No' to liking and 'No' to disliking) for semantically opposite instructions about the same user-item pair.
definitions.paper: SCRIBE: Structured Chain Reasoning for Interactive Behaviour Explanations using Tool Calling

characterization: The phenomenon of producing different, often incorrect, answers across multiple attempts at solving the same problem under identical conditions.
